events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need answer JSON only. Need diagnose most likely fault from evidence: "zero vs. span error diagnosis from cal sheet (FD+DOC)". Scenario: commercial construction, calibration I&C, in-service. Observed field condition: zero vs span error diagnosis from cal sheet (FD+DOC). Need decide pass/fail/needs_more_info, risk, conditions, etc. We need infer likely fault: zero and span errors on calibration sheet indicate instrument out of calibration, likely drift or failed sensor/transmitter. Since in-service, this is a failed/degraded component, non-compliant. Decision likely fail because calibration out of tolerance in service. Need provide findings, actions, rationale, references. Need fill numeric fields: percent_complete maybe 100 (in-service), value maybe 0? Not asked numeric reading. Could set value 0. confidence high 0.9. event_time 0, rate 0, order empty, workable empty, sound_source empty. component_conditions: degraded, failed, non-compliant? The allowed list includes installed-defective, non-compliant, worn, degraded, failed. Use degraded, failed, non-compliant. Risk high or critical? In-service instrument with zero/span error could cause control issues; high. Work_stage in-service. Findings: calibration sheet shows zero and span errors exceeding tolerance; instrument drift/failure. Actions: remove from service, recalibrate/replace, verify loop, update records, retest. Rationale: zero vs span errors indicate out-of-tolerance calibration; in-service asset not meeting ISA and site QC. References: ISA-51.1? Maybe ISA standards for instrumentation symbols, ISA-84? Calibration standards: ISA-RP-12.6? Could cite ISA standards generally and site QC calibration tolerances. Need ensure JSON valid. No prose outside.

events.label: response
events.text:
{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "high",
  "work_stage": "in-service",
  "component_conditions": ["non-compliant", "degraded", "failed"],
  "percent_complete": 100,
  "value": 0,
  "sound_source": "",
  "confidence": 0.9,
  "event_time": 0,
  "rate": 0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Calibration sheet shows both zero and span errors out of tolerance",
    "Instrument is in service while calibration is suspect or invalid",
    "Likely drift or failure of sensor/transmitter causing inaccurate measurement"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Remove instrument from active control loop or flag as out-of-service",
    "Perform full zero and span calibration per manufacturer and site QC procedure",
    "Replace sensor/transmitter if calibration cannot be brought within tolerance",
    "Verify loop wiring, signal conditioning, and process connection",
    "Update calibration records and obtain QC/inspection sign-off before returning to service"
  ],
  "rationale": "A calibration sheet with zero versus span errors indicates the instrument is out of calibration and not reading accurately. Because the device is already in service, it is a non-compliant, degraded or failed asset that can compromise control loop performance, product quality, or safety.",
  "references": [
    "ISA standards for instrument calibration and loop verification",
    "Site QC calibration tolerance and documentation requirements"
  ]
}
